scope.focus: Classify request-prep stack argument 7 sources before it is stored into controller +0xB8.

confirmed_field_role.writer: 0x14015459F loads request-prep stack argument 7 from [RSP+0x80], and 0x1401545A7 stores AL into controller +0xB8.
confirmed_field_role.consumer: 0x140154CD5..0x140154CE7 reads +0xB8 only after confirming len(controller +0xA0) == 6. Nonzero selects packet selector 1; zero selects packet selector 3.
confirmed_field_role.important_negative_result: +0xB8 is not the TOTP map lookup result. The map lookup writes +0xB9/+0xBC for selector 0; +0xB8 is staged earlier by request-prep wrappers.

source_classes.source: Immediate zero is written to request-prep stack argument 7 before 0x140154470 is called.
source_classes.packet_impact: If the installed +0xA0 string is six characters, these paths always emit selector 3 and the compact three-byte parsed code payload.

source_classes.source: The mode byte is forwarded from a higher-level wrapper argument or preserved register, observed through AL/DIL/SIL or original stack-argument aliases.
source_classes.packet_impact: These wrappers let the caller choose selector 1 versus selector 3 for a six-character +0xA0 auth code.

source_classes.source: All three calls read [RBP+0x7F] into AL before writing request-prep stack argument 7. With the wrapper prologue at 0x140153190, [RBP+0x7F] aliases the wrapper stack frame and needs one more upstream pass to name precisely.
source_classes.packet_impact: These calls still behave as dynamic selector 1/3 paths; the packet helper consumes an already-staged mode byte.

source_classes.source: 0x1401F5740 compares dword [R8 + RAX*4 + 0x100] against 1, and 0x1401F574D uses SETZ BPL. That BPL value is forwarded as request-prep stack argument 7.
source_classes.packet_impact: This helper selects selector 1 when the table slot equals 1; otherwise it selects selector 3 when +0xA0 has length six.

callsite_classification.plus_0xB8_source: AL loaded from [RSP+0xA0], an original caller stack-argument alias after SUB RSP, 0x78

callsite_classification.plus_0xA0_source: stack-local string object at [RBP+0x1F], with caller string retained in RSI
stack-local branch string after the value 4/8/9 and 0x14029C950 path

callsite_classification.plus_0xB8_source: AL loaded from [RSP+0xA0], an original caller stack-argument alias after PUSH RDI; SUB RSP, 0x70

callsite_classification.plus_0xA0_source: RSI forwarded into request-prep stack argument 6
RSI forwarded from wrapper incoming R8
callsite_classification.plus_0xB8_source: DIL, set from wrapper incoming R9B at 0x1401541F8

callsite_classification.plus_0xA0_source: RSI forwarded from wrapper incoming R8
callsite_classification.plus_0xB8_source: DIL, set from wrapper incoming R9B at 0x1401541F8
BPL from SETZ after comparing [R8 + RAX*4 + 0x100] with 1

opennxt_947_impact: Selector 1 versus selector 3 is controlled by a setup-time mode byte, not by the six-character code string itself.

OpenNXT compatibility tests should cover selector 1 and selector 3 as separate six-character auth-code packet forms, because some client paths force selector 3 while others let a caller or table decide.
The table-derived path at 0x1401F5731 provides the first concrete nontrivial selector 1/3 decision: table slot equals 1 means selector 1, otherwise selector 3.
The existing selector-0 cached-dword path remains separate and still depends on +0xB9/+0xBC from the TOTP map lookup chain.
next_targets: Resolve the [RBP+0x7F] wrapper-local byte in 0x14015318F..0x14015368B to determine which caller or UI/auth state sets it.
Identify the table object and index source used by 0x1401F5740 when deriving BPL from [R8 + RAX*4 + 0x100].
Correlate selector 1 and selector 3 with OpenNXT 947 auth decoder fixtures so server handling can distinguish both forms cleanly.
Continue mapping the upstream source that becomes controller +0xBC for selector 0.
